phase3_findings.description: arm21b mutation pin used /multi=yes/d which deletes the multi=yes line, leaving elif...then immediately before fi (bash parse error). Pin flipped RED because the script crashed at parse time, not because multi-file detection was behaviorally disabled — degenerated behavioral-fix pin into a removal pin.

arm23 empty-token asserted only the generic ::warning::refresh-app-credentials prefix, not the guard's own message 'mint: access token missing from response' — weak guard signal (shape-3 sibling of the Important pin).

gh-fresh.sh buffers the wrapped gh call's stderr and replays it only after the child exits; removes live stderr diagnostics for a slow/hanging gh call. Partly by-design (buffer needed to scan for 401).

Start step launches refresher only via vendored path with no repo-path fallback (unlike Stop step) and logs 'started' unconditionally; on a self-repo run where the vendored copy is absent the refresher silently never launches. Bounded loud degrade (absent pidfile -> stop-refresher warns, gh-fresh degrades).

gh-fresh.sh header lines 29-30 two-way SUBSTITUTE/DEFER summary omits decide()'s third defer branch (could-not-establish-comparison). Authoritative inline docstring documents it; only the header summary is incomplete.

gh-fresh.sh bad-credential scan tags any 401/Bad credentials/Authentication failed as expired-credential; a genuine non-expiry permission 401 also gets tagged, which can steer the two-strikes fail-fast prose to a misdiagnosed terminal status. Reasoned tradeoff in the comment.
A few defensive real_mint guards remain unexercised (empty-app_id, empty-KEY, empty-signature, surface-2 rename-failure branch). Low value; app_id guard unreachable in production.

fix_decisions.claim_text: stderr buffered-then-replayed for every wrapped gh call
fix_decisions.evidence: Suggestion severity (below fix threshold 'important'); partly by-design — stderr must be captured to scan for the 401 signature. Parked for human review.

fix_decisions.claim_text: Start step lacks the Stop step's repo-path fallback + optimistic launch log
fix_decisions.evidence: Suggestion severity; bounded loud degrade (absent pidfile -> stop-refresher defeat warning, gh-fresh breadcrumb). Parked.

fix_decisions.claim_text: header DEFER enumeration omits third branch
fix_decisions.evidence: Suggestion severity; authoritative inline decide() docstring documents all three branches. Parked.
Suggestion severity; reasoned tradeoff in the comment (favor not missing a real expiry). Parked.
Suggestion severity; guards are defensive/unreachable-in-production. Parked.

phase3_findings.description: docs/cloud-setup.md:268-269 absolute claim 'a failed cycle leaves the previous credential in place' is contradicted by the PR's own surface-1/surface-2 divergence code (refresh-app-credentials.sh:253-265): on a surface-2 write/rename failure, surface-1 is already fresh, so the surfaces diverge. NEW finding — not raised in iter-1.
arm1 asserts only the generic ::warning::refresh-app-credentials prefix, not the specific 'DEVFLOW_APP_ID empty' guard message; loose assertion (shape-3 sibling). NEW.

untested defensive branches: real_mint no-stdin-key guard (119), run_cycle empty-token guard (231), surface-2 rename-failure branch (264-265, only count-pinned), pidfile-write-failure warning (285). Overlaps iter-1 F-7.
gh-fresh.sh buffers wrapped-gh stderr, replays after exit (overlaps iter-1 F-3).
gh-fresh.sh header DEFER enumeration omits third branch (overlaps iter-1 F-5).
